intent_summary: Concise statement of the original PR intent as user-visible behavior, integration outcome, or maintainability outcome. Do not encode exact patch shape.

must_satisfy.requirement: <outcome requirement derived from the original PR intent>
must_satisfy.observable_outcome: <what a reviewer should see in the candidate output if this is satisfied>
must_satisfy.judge_notes: Recognize clean equivalent implementations. Do not require file names, helper names, method names, call order, or module decomposition unless they are explicitly required by public task text or repo-discoverable behavior.

task_rubric_sufficiency.notes: Judges should mark rubric_shape_biased_needs_revise when the supplement would require implementation shape instead of outcomes. Shape-biased requirements must not drive hard rejection.

verification_evidence_suggestions.suggestion: <evidence that would increase confidence; not a mandatory exact-file, exact-helper, or exact-API-shape requirement>

implementation_shape_non_requirements: Do not require byte-for-byte gold patch equivalence.
Do not require exact helper names, file names, module boundaries, call order, or internal decomposition unless public task text or repo-discoverable behavior explicitly requires them.
Different clean designs are acceptable when they satisfy the observable outcome and integration contract.
accepted_variations: Clean alternate implementations are acceptable when they satisfy the task goal and repo-discoverable contracts, even if module boundaries, helper names, or internal decomposition differ from the original PR.
privacy_notes: This supplement is judge-private and must not be shown to candidate agents before candidate output is frozen.
Public reports should summarize outcomes without raw gold/reference content unless explicitly approved.
